title: glochidion cordatum
intro: Glochidion cordatum, also known as the Himalayan milk bush, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a shrub native to the Himalayan region, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and stems, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Glochidion cordatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory problems.
